message.content:
POLARIS MISSION: beat ChatGPT + Gemini + FS-Researcher on deep-research reports.
  DEPTH = process ALL 346 baskets. FAITHFULNESS = context NLI + STRICT numbers.
BINDING INVARIANTS (docs/agentic_outline_redesign.md): faithfulness engine (strict_verify + NLI +
numeric + provenance) is the ONLY hard gate; external content re-enters ONLY via the fold-in seam;
computed numbers render ONLY via the verified [#calc:] lane (never [CITE:ev_xxx]); exploratory
execute_python is BARRED from rendering; weight-and-consolidate, never filter.
FENCE: work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree or the
operator's /home/polaris/polaris_project monitoring dir. Report REAL output only; never fake a pass.


YOU ARE THE BUILDER (Opus hands) for "compose". Fable already found the problems and
told you EXACTLY what to change. DO NOT re-investigate, DO NOT hunt for an entrypoint, DO NOT wander.
Apply ONLY these fixes to src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py, in order:
[
  {
    "severity": "P1",
    "file_line": "src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py:271-283",
    "problem": "async coherence_rewrite_section calls the SYNC verify_sentence_provenance per sentence in a bare for-loop — serial AND on the event loop (default-ON via PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS), freezing all other sections for N_sentences x ~5-9s during every coherence pass.",
    "fix": "Hoist the whole re-verify sweep into one `await asyncio.to_thread(...)`; inside, use a bounded ThreadPoolExecutor over only the changed sentences (new != orig). Same acceptance discipline as fix #1; verify semantics untouched.",
    "acceptance": "Event-loop heartbeat gap stays < 2s during the coherence pass; only changed sentences trigger judge calls (log count matches diff count); re-verify verdicts identical to serial control."
  }
]

For each: make the concrete change at the file:line; then ACTUALLY EXERCISE the touched path (run it,
py_compile is NOT enough — the acceptance observable tells you what to run); then git commit in
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix with an honest message (say PARTIAL if partial). Append one dated line to
/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Report what landed vs what Fable asked. A skipped fix reported is fine; hidden is not.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

bot/compose-fix
0615bc5 compose: unblock the event loop — NLI verify off-loop via asyncio.to_thread
eff82fb compose gear-loop iter 5: GEAR-RULE newest-cp4+cp3 auto-resolve launcher + 429 self-contention concurrency cut (builds on iter-4 resume)
4e0b1f9 compose gear-loop iter 4: section-level crash RESUME (SHA-gated) + self-relaunch launcher

180	        title = " ".join(str(section_context.get("title", "") or "").split())
181	        focus = " ".join(str(section_context.get("focus", "") or "").split())
182	        if title or focus:
183	            lines.append(f'Section title: "{title}"  focus: "{focus}"')
184	            lines.append("")
185	    lines.append("Rewrite these sentences to resolve dangling references, keeping order/count/numbers/tokens:")
186	    lines.append("")
187	    for i, s in enumerate(sentences, start=1):
188	        lines.append(f"{i}. {s}")
189	    return "\n".join(lines)
190	
191	
192	_LEAD_ENUM_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*\d+[.)]\s*")
193	
194	
195	async def _call_coherence_rewrite(sentences: list[str], section_context: "dict | None") -> str:
196	    from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
197	
198	    model = _resolve_model()
199	    max_tokens = _env_int(_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS_ENV, _DEFAULT_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS)
200	    reasoning = _env_int(_COHERENCE_REASONING_ENV, _DEFAULT_COHERENCE_REASONING)
201	    reasoning_arg = reasoning if reasoning and reasoning > 0 else None
202	    client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
203	    try:
204	        response = await client.generate(
205	            prompt=_build_coherence_prompt(sentences, section_context),
206	            system=_COHERENCE_SYSTEM,
207	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
208	            temperature=0.2,
209	            reasoning_max_tokens=reasoning_arg,
210	        )
211	        return str(getattr(response, "content", "") or "")
212	    finally:
213	        try:
214	            await client.close()
215	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — best-effort teardown
216	            pass
217	
218	
219	async def coherence_rewrite_section(
220	    raw: str,
221	    evidence_pool: dict,
222	    *,
223	    section_context: "dict | None" = None,
224	) -> str:
225	    """Fix 4 (P1-2): resolve dangling anaphora in the composed section body ``raw`` (which still carries
226	    ``[#ev:...]`` tokens) BEFORE the unchanged strict_verify tail runs. FAIL-OPEN + kill-switched: on any
227	    fault, a shape/token/count mismatch, or a rewritten sentence that fails the base verifier, the
228	    ORIGINAL sentence is kept — so the pass can only improve coherence or no-op, never lose verified
229	    content or a citation. Returns the (possibly) rewritten body; byte-identical ``raw`` when OFF."""
230	    if not coherence_pass_enabled():
231	        return raw
232	    body = raw or ""
233	    if not body.strip():
234	        return raw
235	    try:
236	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
237	            verify_sentence_provenance,
238	        )
239	        from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
240	            split_into_sentences,
241	        )
242	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
243	        return raw
244	    sentences = split_into_sentences(body)
245	    # Only sentences carrying a provenance token participate; a body with <2 real sentences has no
246	    # cross-sentence antecedent to resolve.
247	    if len([s for s in sentences if _EV_TOKEN_RE.search(s)]) < 2:
248	        return raw
249	    deadline = _env_float(_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_ENV, _DEFAULT_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_S)
250	    try:
251	        import asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
252	
253	        rewritten_text = await asyncio.wait_for(
254	            _call_coherence_rewrite(sentences, section_context), timeout=deadline
255	        )
256	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — model / timeout fault => keep original body (fail-open)
257	        logger.warning("[section_polish] coherence rewrite call failed (fail-open, body unchanged)")
258	        return raw
259	    if not rewritten_text.strip():
260	        return raw
261	    new_sentences = [
262	        _LEAD_ENUM_RE.sub("", ln).strip()
263	        for ln in rewritten_text.splitlines()
264	        if ln.strip()
265	    ]
266	    # SHAPE GUARD: the rewrite must return the SAME number of sentences (no merge/split/reorder handling).
267	    if len(new_sentences) != len(sentences):
268	        return raw
269	    out: list[str] = []
270	    changed = 0
271	    for orig, new in zip(sentences, new_sentences):
272	        orig = orig.strip()
273	        new = new.strip()
274	        if not new or new == orig:
275	            out.append(orig)
276	            continue
277	        # TOKEN GUARD: exact same provenance tokens in the same order (no citation drift).
278	        if _ev_token_multiset(new) != _ev_token_multiset(orig):
279	            out.append(orig)
280	            continue
281	        # BASE-BAR RE-VERIFY: context NLI entailment + forward numeric match. Fail => keep original.
282	        try:
283	            res = verify_sentence_provenance(new, evidence_pool)
284	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — verifier fault on this sentence => keep original
285	            out.append(orig)
286	            continue
287	        if bool(getattr(res, "is_verified", False)):
288	            out.append(str(getattr(res, "sentence", "") or new).strip() or orig)
289	            changed += 1
290	        else:
291	            out.append(orig)
292	    if changed:
293	        logger.info("[section_polish] coherence rewrite resolved referents in %d sentence(s)", changed)
294	    return " ".join(s for s in out if s)
295	
296	
297	# ── Fix 3 (P1-1) — semantic duplicate judge for an appended unit ───────────────────────────────────
298	_DEDUP_SYSTEM = (
299	    "You compare a candidate sentence against a body of report sentences. Answer with a single word — "
300	    "YES or NO. Answer YES only if the candidate states the SAME finding (same claim about the same "
301	    "subject with the same numbers/direction) as at least one sentence already in the body, i.e. it "
302	    "would be redundant to add it. Answer NO if the candidate adds a genuinely different finding, a "
303	    "different subject, a different number, or an opposing/limiting qualification. When unsure, answer NO."
304	)
305	
306	
307	async def sentence_semantically_duplicates(candidate: str, kept_body: str) -> bool:
308	    """Fix 3 (P1-1): True iff ``candidate`` (a boundary / disclosure sentence about to be appended)
309	    states the SAME finding as some sentence already in ``kept_body`` — so the caller can DROP the
310	    duplicate. Semantic (an LLM yes/no judge), not a string match. FAIL-OPEN: any judge error, empty
311	    input, timeout, or non-YES answer returns False (KEEP the unit). Never raises."""
312	    if not boundary_semantic_dedup_enabled():
313	        return False
314	    cand = _strip_tokens(candidate)
315	    body = _strip_tokens(kept_body)
316	    if not cand or not body:
317	        return False
318	    try:
319	        import asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
320	
321	        from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
322	
323	        model = _resolve_model()
324	        deadline = _env_float(_DEDUP_DEADLINE_ENV, _DEFAULT_DEDUP_DEADLINE_S)
325	        prompt = (
326	            "BODY (existing report sentences):\n" + body[:6000] + "\n\n"
327	            "CANDIDATE (a sentence being considered for addition):\n" + cand[:1500] + "\n\n"
328	            "Does the CANDIDATE state the same finding as any sentence already in the BODY? YES or NO."
329	        )
330	        client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
331	        try:
332	            response = await asyncio.wait_for(
333	                client.generate(prompt=prompt, system=_DEDUP_SYSTEM, max_tokens=8, temperature=0.0),
334	                timeout=deadline,
335	            )
336	        finally:
337	            try:
338	                await client.close()
339	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
340	                pass
341	        answer = str(getattr(response, "content", "") or "").strip().lower()
342	        return answer.startswith("yes")
343	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — judge fault => keep the unit (fail-open)
344	        logger.warning("[section_polish] semantic-dup judge failed (fail-open, unit kept)")
345	        return False
346

content.content:
commit 0615bc5a6220e8e8c1b2ba96dc2297940c0ede7c
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sat Jul 11 14:23:29 2026 +0000

    compose: unblock the event loop — NLI verify off-loop via asyncio.to_thread
    
    _draft_passes_wrapper is a sync def calling writer_verify_fn -> entailment_judge ->
    fut.result(timeout), which BLOCKS the calling thread. It was called with no await from
    inside the async coroutine _pre_pass_one_basket, so every NLI judge POST froze the whole
    compose event loop: achieved verify concurrency was 1 regardless of
    PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY (12) or PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM (16).
    
    The real compose ceiling was therefore NOT the OpenRouter 429 -- it was this
    self-inflicted serialization. Both call sites now go through asyncio.to_thread.
    
    Fable gate (independent, empirical A/B, 4 baskets): HEAD 2.06s fully serialized, verify on
    MainThread, heartbeat task starved throughout (loop frozen). Fixed 0.60s parallel, verify on
    asyncio_0..3, max heartbeat gap 0.050s (loop live). SIGN_OFF, 0 P0/P1.
    
    Judge machinery was already built for this: judge_verdict_cache is RLock-guarded and
    documents itself as shared across ThreadPool verifier workers; the judge holds per-thread
    httpx clients via threading.local; POSTs are bounded by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY.
    Real judge concurrency is now capped by that knob (default 4, env-tunable, 0=unbounded)
    instead of by the loop.

 src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py | 12 ++++++++++--
 1 file changed, 10 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)
=====DIFF=====
commit 0615bc5a6220e8e8c1b2ba96dc2297940c0ede7c
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sat Jul 11 14:23:29 2026 +0000

    compose: unblock the event loop — NLI verify off-loop via asyncio.to_thread
    
    _draft_passes_wrapper is a sync def calling writer_verify_fn -> entailment_judge ->
    fut.result(timeout), which BLOCKS the calling thread. It was called with no await from
    inside the async coroutine _pre_pass_one_basket, so every NLI judge POST froze the whole
    compose event loop: achieved verify concurrency was 1 regardless of
    PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_CONCURRENCY (12) or PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM (16).
    
    The real compose ceiling was therefore NOT the OpenRouter 429 -- it was this
    self-inflicted serialization. Both call sites now go through asyncio.to_thread.
    
    Fable gate (independent, empirical A/B, 4 baskets): HEAD 2.06s fully serialized, verify on
    MainThread, heartbeat task starved throughout (loop frozen). Fixed 0.60s parallel, verify on
    asyncio_0..3, max heartbeat gap 0.050s (loop live). SIGN_OFF, 0 P0/P1.
    
    Judge machinery was already built for this: judge_verdict_cache is RLock-guarded and
    documents itself as shared across ThreadPool verifier workers; the judge holds per-thread
    httpx clients via threading.local; POSTs are bounded by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY.
    Real judge concurrency is now capped by that knob (default 4, env-tunable, 0=unbounded)
    instead of by the loop.

diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py
index e79ccfc..cbd8a39 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/abstractive_writer.py
@@ -827,7 +827,12 @@ async def _pre_pass_one_basket(
                 )
                 break
             last_draft = draft
-            passed, reasons = _draft_passes_wrapper(draft, basket, evidence_pool, writer_verify_fn)
+            # BLOCKING: writer_verify_fn -> entailment_judge -> fut.result() waits on the calling
+            # thread. Called inline it stalls the whole event loop, serializing every section's
+            # writer calls behind one NLI POST. Must stay off-loop.
+            passed, reasons = await asyncio.to_thread(
+                _draft_passes_wrapper, draft, basket, evidence_pool, writer_verify_fn,
+            )
             if passed:
                 return draft
             revise_reasons = reasons or None
@@ -903,7 +908,10 @@ async def _pre_pass_one_basket(
             )
             break
         last_draft = draft
-        passed, reasons = _draft_passes_wrapper(draft, basket, evidence_pool, writer_verify_fn)
+        # BLOCKING (see the non-transport-aware branch): keep off the event loop.
+        passed, reasons = await asyncio.to_thread(
+            _draft_passes_wrapper, draft, basket, evidence_pool, writer_verify_fn,
+        )
         if passed:
             return draft
         revise_reasons = reasons or None

1	"""Post-compose SECTION-POLISH passes (2026-07-10 compose gear-loop iter 2).
2	
3	Three question-agnostic, FAIL-OPEN, kill-switched passes that improve the visible quality of an
4	already-strict_verify-passed section WITHOUT touching the faithfulness engine. Every rewritten or
5	synthesized sentence is RE-VERIFIED by the base bar (``verify_sentence_provenance`` — context-level
6	NLI entailment + forward numeric match); on any failure the ORIGINAL is kept and the pass no-ops.
7	
8	  * :func:`synthesize_qualifier_sentence` — Fix 1 (P0-1). ONE clean LLM-synthesized sentence from a
9	    boundary QUALIFIER basket (via the same abstractive writer + base verify bar), so the boundary
10	    line renders synthesis, never a raw span quote. "" when the writer produced nothing or the draft
11	    failed verify (the caller then emits no boundary line — never a raw quote).
12	  * :func:`coherence_rewrite_section` — Fix 4 (P1-2). A section-level referent-naming rewrite that
13	    resolves dangling anaphora ("these estimates" / "the researchers" with no antecedent) by naming
14	    the referent, keeping every number and every ``[#ev:...]`` token character-for-character and the
15	    sentence order fixed. Each rewritten sentence is re-verified; a failing or token-altering rewrite
16	    keeps the ORIGINAL sentence (never loses verified content).
17	  * :func:`sentence_semantically_duplicates` — Fix 3 (P1-1). A bounded semantic-equivalence judge:
18	    does a candidate boundary/disclosure sentence state the SAME finding as any of the section's kept
19	    verified sentences? Used to DROP a duplicated appended unit. FAIL-OPEN — any judge error / doubt
20	    returns False (keep the unit).
21	
22	All three are DEFAULT-ON kill-switches (LAW VI). The faithfulness engine (verify_sentence_provenance
23	/ strict_verify / provenance / D8) is UNTOUCHED — these passes only propose; the base verifier gates.
24	"""
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import logging
28	import os
29	import re
30	from typing import Any, Optional
31	
32	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
33	
34	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")
35	
36	# ── env knobs (LAW VI) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
37	_COHERENCE_ENV = "PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS"
38	_BOUNDARY_DEDUP_ENV = "PG_BOUNDARY_SEMANTIC_DEDUP"
39	_MODEL_ENV = "PG_ABSTRACTIVE_WRITER_MODEL"
40	_DEFAULT_MODEL = "z-ai/glm-5.2"
41	_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS_ENV = "PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS"
42	_DEFAULT_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS = 32768
43	_COHERENCE_REASONING_ENV = "PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_REASONING_MAX_TOKENS"
44	_DEFAULT_COHERENCE_REASONING = 16384
45	_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_ENV = "PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_S"
46	_DEFAULT_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_S = 180.0
47	_DEDUP_DEADLINE_ENV = "PG_BOUNDARY_DEDUP_DEADLINE_S"
48	_DEFAULT_DEDUP_DEADLINE_S = 60.0
49	
50	
51	def _flag_on(name: str, default_on: bool = True) -> bool:
52	    raw = os.getenv(name, "1" if default_on else "0").strip().lower()
53	    if default_on:
54	        return raw not in ("0", "false", "off", "no")
55	    return raw in ("1", "true", "on", "yes")
56	
57	
58	def coherence_pass_enabled() -> bool:
59	    """``PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_PASS`` kill-switch (default ON). OFF => no rewrite, byte-identical body."""
60	    return _flag_on(_COHERENCE_ENV, default_on=True)

77:def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
87:def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
97:def _ev_token_multiset(text: str) -> tuple:
199:    max_tokens = _env_int(_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS_ENV, _DEFAULT_COHERENCE_MAX_TOKENS)
200:    reasoning = _env_int(_COHERENCE_REASONING_ENV, _DEFAULT_COHERENCE_REASONING)
249:    deadline = _env_float(_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_ENV, _DEFAULT_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_S)

content.text: Now let me add the env knob and apply the fix.

input.new_string:
_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_ENV = "PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_S"
_DEFAULT_COHERENCE_DEADLINE_S = 180.0
# Bounded pool width for the off-loop coherence re-verify sweep (fix P1-2). The judge itself is
# already POST-capped by PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY; this just caps how many changed sentences
# verify at once inside the single asyncio.to_thread hop.
_COHERENCE_VERIFY_WORKERS_ENV = "PG_SECTION_COHERENCE_VERIFY_WORKERS"
_DEFAULT_COHERENCE_VERIFY_WORKERS = 4
